label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what we can infer from Toy Fair 2014 based on the given passage. The options are A to D. First, I need to go through the passage again to pick out the relevant information. The passage mentions that the 2014 American International Toy Fair was the biggest in its history and highlights some trends. The main points about the trends are "oversize toys," "Larger Than Life" being a favorite, and the STEAM focus which includes science, tech, engineering, art, and math. It also talks about technology influencing toys, like 3D games and robots. Looking at the options: A) Toys are becoming more educational than fun. The passage says there's a push towards education with STEAM, but it also mentions that it's all fun and games. It doesn't say they are becoming more educational than fun, just that there's an educational aspect. So maybe A isn't correct. B) Robots are great favorites of buyers. The passage mentions that robots were seen, but it's not stated that they were the favorites of buyers. It's part of the trends influenced by tech. So B might not be directly supported. C) Big toys will be popular this year. The passage says that "everything is really big and really out there" according to the expert, and "Larger Than Life" was a favorite. So this directly supports option C. D) Simple card games will disappear on the market.
